build.observation: xcodegen generate + xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Debug build: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED ** with zero warnings. Kite.app signed ad-hoc, Info.plist baked with all VAL-PKG-004 keys (CFBundleIdentifier=nl.rb2.kite, CFBundleName=Kite, LSApplicationCategoryType=public.app-category.developer-tools, LSMinimumSystemVersion=15.0, NSHighResolutionCapable=true).

test.observation: xcodebuild test -scheme Kite -destination 'platform=macOS' -only-testing:KiteTests: 72 tests in 11 suites passed in 12.818s. Suites: BranchParser (7), DiffParser (7), ErrorClassifier (13), FSWatcher (5), ForEachRefParser (6), Git.run / Git.stream (10), LogParser (7), PersistenceStore (9), ProgressParser (8), StatusParser (6), Kite scaffold smoke (2). Per task guidance, KiteUITests was NOT run (blocked by host TCC automation grant).

swiftformat.observation: swiftformat Sources Tests --lint: 0/39 files require formatting; config at .swiftformat honored.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Git.run reads stdout/stderr via readToEnd() in the terminationHandler only — does NOT drain concurrently despite the inline comment claiming otherwise. Outputs >64 KB (git diff on large commits, git show, git log --patch) will pipe-buffer-deadlock the child. Fine for all M1 test callers (small outputs); must be fixed before M7-uncommitted-diff and M7-commit-diff ship. Either refactor Git.run to drain both pipes via readabilityHandler OR require M7 to use Git.stream.

onCancel closure can miss terminating the child if cancel fires between continuation setup and process.run() — the cancelFlag still causes the awaiter to see .cancelled but the subprocess runs to natural completion. M5-net-ops should audit this for fetch/push lifecycle expectations.
GitError lacks a case for push-specific hook rejection / protected-branch / no-such-ref errors that M5-net-ops will need. Add .remoteRejected(String) before M5 lands.

extractDiffGitPaths does not handle git's quoted-path form for filenames with spaces. Known limitation; recommend logging this in library/git-cli-integration.md so M7 picks it up, and emitting a Logger warning at the extract site so silent drops are observable.

consume(_:) only returns the LAST event per chunk — intermediate percent updates are lost to the caller. For M5-fetch driving a smooth progress bar, consider adding consumeAll(_:) -> [ProgressEvent] or switching to a callback/AsyncStream shape.

Uses the Swift 5.10+ if-expression syntax. project.yml sets SWIFT_VERSION=5.9 but Xcode 16's toolchain ships Swift 5.10, so it compiles today. Bump project.yml to 5.10 or rewrite as ternary for strict-5.9 portability.
RefKind.head case is defined but never produced (ForEachRefParser filters HEAD out). Remove or document the intended consumer.

9 per-field persistence keys declared but unused. If a future feature binds via @AppStorage to any of them, it will drift from the aggregate blob silently. Pick a strategy (remove the unused keys OR write both) and document in AGENTS.md before M5-auto-fetch.
load()/write() swallow errors silently with only a code comment. Add os.Logger.error calls so corrupt-blob recovery and encode failures are observable in Console. Zero cost when they don't fire.
setWindowFrame saves on every call. Window resize produces many calls per second. Debounce before M8 binds this to live window-resize events.

stop() is terminal — start() on a stopped watcher is a silent no-op. Surprising API; prefer throwing WatcherError.alreadyStopped so a caller that hits this gets a loud signal rather than a missed refresh.

@unchecked Sendable justification is not in a doc-comment. Defensible choice over an actor (C callback can't await into one), but the next reviewer shouldn't have to re-litigate. One sentence explaining the tradeoff.

Documents the old swiftformat flag order (`swiftformat --lint Sources Tests`). swiftformat 0.61+ requires paths before flags. Handoff flagged this for fix but README was not updated. Every future worker copying from README will hit it.

EmptyCommands replaces .newItem without offering a replacement. Silently removes the default ⌘N binding that VAL-UI-009 depends on. M8-commands-and-menu must restore it. Leave a comment pointer so M8 doesn't miss it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add a section under Conventions → Naming: 'The Codable persistence root is `KiteSettings`, not `Settings`, because SwiftUI's `Settings` scene type is imported by KiteApp.swift and collides. When adding any Settings-related view types in later features, use `SettingsRootsTab`, `SettingsGeneralTab`, etc. rather than bare `SettingsView` to avoid compounding the clash.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M1-persistence hit this and renamed; M2-settings-roots will hit it again for the real Settings scene body. Codify now.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add a known gotcha under 'Git CLI': 'Git.run captures stdout/stderr only after process termination via readToEnd(). This is safe for any git command whose combined output stays under ~64 KB (pipe-buffer size). For large-output commands — `git diff`, `git show`, `git log --patch`, `git archive` — use Git.stream or refactor Git.run to drain pipes via readabilityHandler. Do NOT assume Git.run scales to diff-sized outputs.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Latent deadlock for any M7 diff caller. Document the boundary until Git.run is reworked.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add to Services or Shared state rules: 'Whenever a feature swallows an error (corrupt UserDefaults, failed JSON encode, file-system missing parent, etc.), log it via `os.Logger(subsystem: "nl.rb2.kite", category: <area>).error(...)` so the failure is observable. Silent swallows are never acceptable; swallow + log is.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: PersistenceStore swallows twice today with only comments. The precedent will spread unless codified.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Document the 'stateless parser = enum, stateful stream consumer = final class' pattern used across M1-git-parsers. Example: BranchParser / LogParser / StatusParser / ForEachRefParser / DiffParser are enums with static parse(); ProgressParser is a final class because of carry-buffer + last-emitted dedup across chunks. Codify so M4-graph-layout (stateless) / M5 progress parser extensions / M6 branch-name validator pick the right shape.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Five features will produce parsers / validators in coming milestones. Without a pattern, they drift.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add a 'Known parser limitations' subsection: (1) DiffParser does not handle git's quoted-path form for filenames with embedded spaces — a quoted `diff --git "a/foo bar" "b/foo bar"` header causes the file-path extraction to silently return nil and the file's paths stay unset. M7-uncommitted-diff and M7-commit-diff must extend DiffParser.extractDiffGitPaths before shipping in repos with space-in-filename diffs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Deferred work needs a parking spot so M7 workers don't rediscover it at runtime.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add under §4 Shared state rules: 'FSWatcher instances are one-shot — call start() once, stop() once. Do not attempt to start a stopped watcher. When switching focused repos, create a fresh FSWatcher rather than restarting the existing one.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: FSWatcher.start() is a silent no-op after stop(). M3-repo-focus-lifecycle (the primary consumer) needs to know this explicitly.

anyOrphansOrFailures.note: M1-fs-watcher provides the refresh-event mechanism, but scroll-position preservation is owned by M4-graph-scroll-container. Listed as partial in the feature handoff, correctly.

grep over Sources/ for `force`, `--force`, `--force-with-lease` returns zero hits in any Process argument list. Explicit VAL-SEC-001 fulfillment is scoped to M5-pull-push per features.json but the invariant holds at M1 (there is simply no push code yet to offend it).
